prototypes.notes: Zero-dep fs.mkdirSync mutex proven sufficient for concurrent append safety: 2 writers x 1000 events with zero duplicates, torn writes, or lost events. EventEnvelope discriminated union provides compile-time exhaustiveness on all 3 PoC variants. foldToSnapshot() fold confirms O(n) Reducer LOGIC pattern for LEARN-domain BackwardProp.

prototypes.empiricalVerdict: Snapshot + StaleObject LOSES 484 updates out of 2000 (24.2% loss rate) under 2-writer concurrent adversarial race. StaleObject detects 111 conflicts but the read-modify-write pattern inherently allows lost updates when writers don't retry. No lineage preserved across versions. Empirically confirms simulator flag-02 MEDIUM: H-B IRRECONCILABLE with world-model LEARN-centrality. H-A's 0 lost events vs H-B's 484 lost updates = decisive architectural defeat.
prototypes.notes: 316 LOC (exceeded 100-200 target but tests pass + tsc clean). Race-worker pattern uses Bun Worker to spawn concurrent writers. File-based snapshot with version field matches Palantir StaleObject pattern per validation/README.md §VAL.R-07, but append-only is strictly superior for LEARN-centric ontology architecture.

comparisonSummary.criticalMetric: Lost updates under 2-writer adversarial race (flag-01 HIGH severity from simulator)
comparisonSummary.verdict: H-A: 0 lost events / 2000 (0.0%) -- H-B: 484 lost updates / 2000 (24.2%)

architecturalImplications.winner: Append-Only Event Log (events.jsonl + sequence counter + fs.mkdir mutex) — production-safe, LEARN-first, matches Anthropic Managed Agents + Palantir Decision Lineage + OMC clawhip three-way convergence
architecturalImplications.loser: Snapshot + StaleObject — works for single-writer systems with in-place mutation, but loses updates under concurrent writes without explicit retry loops, and destroys lineage

comparisonSummary.evaluatorRecommendation: R14 Prototype Build Failure check: both prototypes buildStatus=success (no R14 trigger). R15 Eval Pass Rate: H-A 5/5 pass (100%), H-B 5/5 pass (100%) — both above 0.5 threshold (no R15 trigger). However, H-B's empirical weakness (484 lost updates) should factor into final scoring even though it passes formal pass-rate. The 24.2% update loss is a qualitative disqualifier under R11 (D/L/A Fit — LEARN domain cannot be reliably populated from a lossy substrate).

evalSuites.label: Append-Only Event Log Foundation — eval-runner T9

cases.name: append 10 events and read back with exact sequence + content
cases.notes: Round-trip: ACTION(append) -> DATA(NDJSON) -> DATA(readEvents). Sequences 1..10 exact, payload content preserved.

cases.name: only valid EventEnvelope variants accepted by the type system
cases.notes: Discriminated union exhaustiveness: switch over 3 variants covers all branches. isValidType guard rejects unknown strings.

cases.name: fold 20-event log into typed count snapshot
cases.notes: foldToSnapshot Reducer: 5 session_started + 5 edit_proposed + 10 edit_committed = 20 total, lastSequence=20.

cases.name: 100 sequential appends produce strictly monotonic sequences 1..100
cases.notes: Monotonicity: no gaps, no duplicates in 1..100 range. Validates prim-action-03 AtomicCommit sequential correctness.

cases.name: 2-writer adversarial race: 2x1000 events, zero duplicates/torn writes/lost events
cases.notes: CRITICAL gap-01 resolution. Promise.all concurrent writers, fs.mkdir mutex proven atomic. 0 lost events / 2000. Reproducible across 2 runs.

cases.name: foldToSnapshot on empty event list yields all-zero snapshot
cases.notes: Edge case: LOGIC reducer must handle empty input. lastSequence=0, all counters 0. Cold-start safety.

cases.name: type guard exhaustiveness — each variant resolves to exactly one guard
cases.notes: isEditProposed/isEditCommitted/isSessionStarted cross-product: 9 assertions. No guard returns true for wrong variant.

cases.name: ACTION->DATA->LOGIC pipeline seam: append->read->fold round-trip
cases.notes: 14-event pipeline (3+7+4 mix). Sequence gaps would indicate integration bug. All 1..14 present. BackwardProp path verified.

cases.name: readEvents on non-existent path returns [] without throwing (cold-start)
cases.notes: Cold-start path: new session with no prior log. readEvents must not throw on missing file. Graceful degradation.

cases.name: stale lock directory causes retry exhaustion with clear error message
cases.notes: Adversarial: orphaned lock from crashed writer. appendEventAtomic must fail after MAX_RETRIES=20 with informative message. No deadlock.

evalSuites.label: Snapshot-based + StaleObject optimistic concurrency — eval-runner T9

cases.name: writes v1 and reads back with correct content and version
cases.notes: Single-writer round-trip. version=1, lastModified ISO string, data fields preserved. No concurrency.

cases.name: updates snapshot from v1 to v2 when expectedVersion matches
cases.notes: Happy path: read v1, write v2 with expectedVersion=1. Version increments correctly. Single-writer only.

cases.name: throws StaleObjectError when concurrent writer has already committed
cases.notes: Two readers of v1; writer A commits v2; writer B throws StaleObjectError. DETECTION works in serialized scenario.

cases.name: v1 and v2 data LOST after v1->v2->v3 overwrites (H-B weakness)
cases.notes: LEARN failure confirmed: after 3 writes, only v3 survives on disk. v1 and v2 states are irrecoverable. BackwardProp structurally broken.

cases.name: adversarial 2-writer race: TOCTOU produces lost updates (H-B defeat)
cases.notes: CRITICAL: test PASSES (assertions verify lost updates > 0). Empirical loss: run1=484, run2=353, run3=56 (TOCTOU variance). All runs confirm lostUpdates > 0.

cases.name: Snapshot schema field invariants on bootstrap (version=1, ISO lastModified, data preserved)
cases.notes: Schema correctness: version increments from 0->1, lastModified is valid ISO8601, data fields round-trip without loss.

cases.name: StaleObjectError carries correct expectedVersion and actualVersion
cases.notes: Error metadata: expectedVersion=1, actualVersion=2 on v1->v2->stale-v1-write. name='StaleObjectError'. Retry caller has correct info.

cases.name: sequential read-modify-write: 10 increments produce counter=10 (single writer)
cases.notes: Best case for H-B: single writer, no concurrency. Correct under sequential load. version=11, counter=10 exactly.

cases.name: StaleObjectError thrown but losing writer's data silently discarded without retry
cases.notes: ACTION failure: +200 write permanently lost when StaleObjectError thrown. Final counter=100, not 300. No built-in retry. Write loss confirmed.

cases.name: LEARN lineage gap: prior versions irrecoverably destroyed after overwrites
cases.notes: LEARN domain critical failure: raw JSON file does NOT contain v1 or v2 state strings. Only v3 survives. prim-learn-01 BackwardProp is structurally impossible from H-B substrate.

failureModes.description: 24.2% empirical update loss rate (484/2000 in authoritative run; 56-484 range across 3 runs). AppendOnlyEventLog (prim-learn-01) cannot be populated from a lossy substrate. BackwardProp path from H-B to ontology is structurally broken.

TOCTOU window between read-check-write in writeSnapshot is non-atomic. Two writers can both pass the version check simultaneously, producing silent lost updates that workers report as successful writes. No built-in retry mechanism.

StaleObjectError detection works in serialized scenarios but is bypassed in true OS-level concurrency when two writers enter the TOCTOU window simultaneously. Detection coverage is incomplete under concurrent load.
No lineage record across versions. Each write overwrites prior state; there is no sidecar log, version history, or event sequence. Prior states are irrecoverable from disk after overwrite.

runSummary.note: TOCTOU window size varies with OS scheduling. All 3 runs confirm lostUpdates > 0. Minimum observed loss: 56/2000 (2.8%). Maximum: 484/2000 (24.2%). The weakness is structural, not load-dependent.

evalRunnerNotes: Both prototypes technically pass all formal tests (H-A 10/10, H-B 10/10 including eval suite). The architecturally decisive finding is QUALITATIVE, not a pass/fail binary: H-B's adversarial test PASSES in the sense that the assertions VERIFY the weakness exists (lostUpdates > 0 is expected and asserted). H-B's failure modes are structural: (1) TOCTOU window produces non-deterministic lost updates under OS-level concurrency, (2) no append-only record means prim-learn-01 (AppendOnlyEventLog) BackwardProp is impossible. These are R11 (D/L/A Fit — LEARN domain cannot be reliably populated) and R13 (BackwardProp path broken) triggers. H-A passes all 10 tests including adversarial race with 0 lost events across 2 independent runs. Recommend evaluator apply nuanced R11/R13/R14/R15 analysis. H-A passRate=1.0 with zero failure modes. H-B passRate=1.0 with 4 classified failure modes (2 critical, 2 high).
